content:
The tutorial trap # Most people start with tutorials. I did too. The problem is you can follow along perfectly and still not know how to build anything on your own.
What changed # The switch happened when I started working on real, small projects — things I actually wanted to exist. Broken code that I had to fix myself. Features I had to research and figure out.
Key things that helped # Git from day one — version control forced me to think in structure Reading docs — not just Stack Overflow Building in public — even just on GitHub Still learning # Always will be. That’s kind of the point.
summary: A breakdown of how I approached learning web dev, and what actually worked.
title: How I Actually Learned Web Development

content:
Why I’m here # I’ve been building things on the web for a while now, mostly quietly. This blog is my attempt to document what I learn, share what I build, and think out loud.
What to expect # Posts here will mostly cover:
Things I’m building or experimenting with Concepts I’ve had to really dig into to understand Occasional opinions on tools and workflows More posts coming soon.
summary: My first post — what this blog is about and what you can expect.
title: Hello World — Why I Started This Blog

content:
Hey, I’m Ansh 👋 # I’m a web developer based in India. I work mainly with web technologies and enjoy building things that are simple, functional, and well thought out.
What I Do # I have experience with frontend and backend basics, version control using Git, and working with real projects rather than just tutorials. I like understanding how things work under the hood — not just using them.
Skills # Frontend: HTML, CSS, JavaScript Backend: Node.js basics, REST APIs Tools: Git, GitHub, Hugo Currently # Always picking up new tools along the way. Check out my work on GitHub.
title: About
